definition: A yellow pigment produced when the liver breaks down old red blood cells. High levels can cause jaundice (yellowing of skin/eyes).
Blood sugar — your body's main energy source. High fasting levels may indicate diabetes or pre-diabetes.
A waste product filtered by your kidneys. Elevated levels suggest your kidneys may not be filtering properly.
The protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen throughout your body. Low levels indicate anemia.
A 3-month average of your blood sugar levels. Used to diagnose and monitor diabetes.
Alanine aminotransferase — a liver enzyme. Elevated levels indicate liver inflammation or damage.
Thyroid Stimulating Hormone — controls how much thyroid hormone your body makes. Abnormal levels indicate thyroid issues.
A protein that stores iron in your body and shows how much iron you have saved.
A vitamin that helps keep your bones strong and supports your immune system.
A hormone that controls how active your thyroid gland is.
An active thyroid hormone that affects metabolism and energy levels.
A thyroid hormone that helps regulate metabolism and body functions.
White blood cells that help your body fight infections.
Red blood cells that carry oxygen throughout your body.
normalRange: Men: 4.7–6.1 million cells/µL, Women: 4.2–5.4 million cells/µL

definition: Cells that help your blood clot and stop bleeding.
A test that shows if there is inflammation in your body.
A protein that increases when there is inflammation or infection.
An electrolyte that helps control fluid balance and nerve function.
An electrolyte important for heart and muscle function.
A mineral needed for strong bones, muscles, and nerves.
A mineral that supports muscle, nerve, and heart function.
A waste product that can build up and cause gout if too high.
A test that measures waste products in your blood to check kidney function.
An estimate of how well your kidneys are filtering blood.
Often called bad cholesterol that can build up in your arteries.
Known as good cholesterol that helps remove excess cholesterol.
A type of fat in your blood used for energy.
An enzyme found in the liver that rises when the liver is damaged.
An enzyme mainly in the liver that indicates liver health.
An enzyme related to liver and bone health.
An enzyme that helps detect liver and bile duct problems.
The total amount of protein in your blood, including albumin and globulin.
A protein made by the liver that helps keep fluid in your bloodstream.
A test that shows how long it takes your blood to clot.
A test measuring how quickly your blood clots.
A test that checks blood clotting through a different pathway.
A test that shows your average blood sugar over the past 2–3 months.
A hormone that helps control blood sugar levels.
A hormone released during stress that affects metabolism and immune response.
A protein produced by the prostate used to screen for prostate issues.
